title: cuscuta glandulosa
intro: Cuscuta glandulosa, also known as Dodder, is a parasitic plant that grows by attaching itself to other plants and obtaining its nutrients through the host plant's vascular system. Traditionally, Cuscuta glandulosa has been used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Cuscuta glandulosa include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, as well as its use as a diuretic and laxative. Cuscuta glandulosa is commonly prepared as a herbal tea or powder, and it can also be consumed as a tincture or capsule, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
